 ## History
 
-The warez culture emerged in the early 1980s with the rise of personal computers and the availability of bulletin board systems (BBS). Early warez groups focused on cracking software protection mechanisms and sharing the cracked software through BBS networks. Over time, the warez scene evolved to include organized groups with hierarchical structures, specialized roles (such as "crackers" who break protection), and distribution methods that utilized FTP sites, IRC channels, and later, peer-to-peer networks.
